title: rubus horridus var. wirtgenii
intro: Rubus horridus var. wirtgenii, also known as the thorny raspberry or Wirtgen's horrid raspberry, is a thorny shrub native to the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its rich content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential anticancer properties. Rubus horridus var. wirtgenii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its leaves and stems are often harvested for use in herbal remedies.
